title: Reorder Levels
description: Manually specify the order of levels in a factor.
problemStatement:
Define the hierarchy! Create a factor from `c("Low", "High", "Medium")` where the levels are explicitly ordered as `"Low"`, `"Medium"`, `"High"`.

**Requirement:** Use the `levels` argument in the `factor()` function.
hints: By default, R orders levels alphabetically. Use `levels=c(...)` to override this.
expectedOutput:
[1] Low    High   Medium
Levels: Low Medium High
solutionCode:
x <- c("Low", "High", "Medium")
f_x <- factor(x, levels = c("Low", "Medium", "High"))
print(f_x)
